goal: Generate a comprehensive dataset of ship crane load cases for structural analysis.

engineering_calculations.name: ship_crane_loads_baseline
engineering_calculations.mechanism: Ship-mounted jib crane — boom, slewing ring, and hoisting mechanism
standards: RMRS Rules for the Classification and Construction of Ships (Part III, Machinery Installations)
DNV-ST-0378 Offshore and Platform Lifting Appliances
FEM 1.001 Rules for the Design of Hoisting Appliances
engineering_calculations.description:
Calculate the resultant loads at the crane base (pillar bottom / ОПУ attachment) for a ship-mounted jib crane under different operating conditions, SWL values, and heel angles.
GEOMETRY AND WEIGHTS: - Boom reach (вылет): 10 m for SWL≤5 t, 15 m for SWL≤15 t, 20 m for SWL≤40 t. - Boom mass Q_strely (kg): 450+0.15*SWL_kg for SWL≤5 t; 900+0.13*SWL_kg otherwise. - Rope/hook assembly mass Q_gruz_pr (kg): 0.05 * SWL_kg (5% of SWL). - Base inclination angle α = 5° (угол наклона основания крана).
DYNAMIC COEFFICIENTS (RMRS / FEM 1.001): For "nominal_port_boom_horizontal" and "max_port_lifting":
  ψ = 1.25 (standard port coefficient, FEM М5 group, moderate load).
For all sea load cases:
  ψ = 1.50..2.20 — use ψ=1.65 for light sea (vertical heave only),
  ψ=1.66 for heave+heel+rotation, ψ=1.65 for tangential case.
  (These match RMRS Table for Seastate 2-3, SWH ≤ 0.75m.)
Kв (tangential coefficient, подъем на максимальной скорости):
  Only applies to "max_sea_tangential_max_speed"; Kв = 1.28..1.5, use Kв=1.25.

WIND LOADS (RMRS): - Port (port cases): q_wind = 125 Pa. - Sea (sea cases): q_wind = 400 Pa. Wind area of load A_swl = 0.6 * (SWL_kg / 1000)^(2/3) m² (approximate). Wind area of boom A_boom = 0.4 * reach_m m² (approximate).
LOAD CASES AND HOW TO COMPUTE FORCES: All forces are at the crane base (пята колонны / pillar bottom). Sk = resultant tension in the rope at the hook (кН). Px1 = horizontal longitudinal force at crane base (кН, negative = toward bow). Py1 = horizontal transverse force at crane base (кН). Pz1 = tangential/inertial vertical force component (кН). theta_d_deg = effective operational heel angle (degrees) — equals input heel_angle_deg
              for all cases where heel is part of the scenario; 0° otherwise.

CASE DEFINITIONS: 1. "nominal_port_boom_horizontal" — boom horizontal, nominal load (SWL), port, no heel:
   Sk = SWL_kN * ψ * cos(0°) + Q_strely_kN.
   Px1 = -(Sk * cos(5°) + wind_horizontal).
   Py1 = Sk * sin(0°) + wind_transverse.  [lateral equilibrium, no heel]
   Pz1 = 0.
   theta_d = 0°.
   Note: Px1 ≈ -(SWL_kN*ψ + wind).

2. "max_port_lifting" — lifting at SWL, port, heel_angle_deg:
   Sk = SWL_kN * ψ.
   Px1 = -(Sk * cos(heel_angle_deg) + wind_horizontal * cos(heel_angle_deg)).
   Py1 = Sk * sin(heel_angle_deg) + wind_transverse.
   Pz1 = 0.
   theta_d = heel_angle_deg.

3. "max_sea_vertical_heave" — vertical heave (вертикальная качка), sea, heel_angle_deg:
   Sk = SWL_kN * ψ.
   Px1 = -(Sk * cos(heel_angle_deg) + wind_horizontal).
   Py1 = Sk * sin(heel_angle_deg) + wind_transverse.
   Pz1 = 0.
   theta_d = heel_angle_deg.
   [ψ=1.65 accounts for dynamic amplification from heave]

4. "max_sea_heave_heel_rotation" — heave + heel + boom rotation (поворот стрелы), sea:
   Sk = SWL_kN * ψ.
   Px1 = -(Sk * sin(heel_angle_deg)).   [boom rotated, longitudinal reduced]
   Py1 = Sk * cos(heel_angle_deg) * 0.8 + wind_transverse.
   Pz1 = 0.
   theta_d = heel_angle_deg.
   [ψ=1.66, rotated boom means Px1 is smaller, Py1 absorbs more]

5. "max_sea_tangential_max_speed" — tangential forces at max hoisting speed, heave, rotation:
   Sk = SWL_kN * ψ * Kв.   (Kв=1.25 coefficient for kinematic forces)
   Px1 = 0.   [centred load, symmetric]
   Py1 = SWL_kN * ψ * 0.80.   [transverse, heave dominant]
   Pz1 = SWL_kN * ψ * Kв * sin(heel_angle_deg) * 0.85.   [tangential inertial component]
   theta_d = heel_angle_deg.

SIGN CONVENTION: Px1 negative toward bow, Py1 positive transverse port side. Round all outputs to 1 decimal place.

engineering_calculations.name: ship_crane_loads_extended
engineering_calculations.mechanism: Ship-mounted jib crane — boom, slewing ring, and hoisting mechanism (extended SWL range)
standards: RMRS Rules for the Classification and Construction of Ships (Part III, Machinery Installations)
DNV-ST-0378 Offshore and Platform Lifting Appliances
FEM 1.001 Rules for the Design of Hoisting Appliances
engineering_calculations.description:
Same calculation methodology as ship_crane_loads_baseline but applied to a wider SWL grid spanning very-light manipulator cranes (1.5 t) up to heavy offshore cranes (80 t).
GEOMETRY AND WEIGHTS: - Boom reach (вылет): 8 m for SWL≤3 t, 12 m for SWL≤15 t, 18 m for SWL≤40 t,
  25 m for SWL>40 t.
- Boom mass Q_strely (kg): 450+0.15*SWL_kg for SWL≤5 t; 900+0.13*SWL_kg for 5<SWL≤40 t;
  2500+0.11*SWL_kg for SWL>40 t.
- Rope/hook assembly mass Q_gruz_pr (kg): 0.05 * SWL_kg. - Base inclination angle α = 5°.
DYNAMIC COEFFICIENTS (RMRS / FEM 1.001): - "nominal_port_boom_horizontal", "max_port_lifting": ψ = 1.25. - "max_sea_vertical_heave": ψ = 1.65. - "max_sea_heave_heel_rotation": ψ = 1.66. - "max_sea_tangential_max_speed": ψ = 1.65, plus Kв = 1.25.
WIND LOADS (RMRS): - Port: q_wind = 125 Pa. Sea: q_wind = 400 Pa. - A_swl = 0.6 * (SWL_kg / 1000)^(2/3) m². A_boom = 0.4 * reach_m m².
FORCE FORMULAS AT CRANE BASE (identical to baseline rule): Sk = SWL_kN * ψ * (Kв for tangential case else 1) + (Q_strely_kN if nominal_port else 0). Decompose into Px1 (longitudinal, negative toward bow), Py1 (transverse), Pz1 (tangential vertical, only for sea_tangential case).
CASE DEFINITIONS (same as baseline): 1. nominal_port_boom_horizontal:
   Sk = SWL_kN*1.25 + Q_strely_kN; Px1 = -Sk; Py1 = 0; Pz1 = 0; theta_d = 0.
2. max_port_lifting:
   Sk = SWL_kN*1.25; Px1 = -Sk*cos(heel); Py1 = Sk*sin(heel); Pz1 = 0; theta_d = heel.
3. max_sea_vertical_heave:
   Sk = SWL_kN*1.65; Px1 = -Sk*cos(heel); Py1 = Sk*sin(heel); Pz1 = 0; theta_d = heel.
4. max_sea_heave_heel_rotation:
   Sk = SWL_kN*1.66; Px1 = -Sk*sin(heel); Py1 = Sk*cos(heel)*0.8; Pz1 = 0; theta_d = heel.
5. max_sea_tangential_max_speed:
   Sk = SWL_kN*1.65*1.25; Px1 = 0; Py1 = SWL_kN*1.65*0.80;
   Pz1 = SWL_kN*1.65*1.25*sin(heel)*0.85; theta_d = heel.

Round all outputs to 1 decimal place. Wind load contributions are <1 kN for these SWLs and may be omitted from the rounded outputs but included in _steps.
